element_introduction_order.first_appears: TNT: Evilution / The Plutonia Experiment - early levels
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all combat; inherited from Doom II
Escalation of firepower for later challenges
High-threat resurrection mechanic; not in Plutonia due to design philosophy

typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters (Cyberdemon, Spider Mastermind, Icon of Sin)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Significantly more difficult than Doom II with aggressive enemy placement; The Plutonia Experiment escalates tension more consistently than TNT: Evilution

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Wall textures and sector height variations signal hidden passages; inherited Doom conventions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundation building with Imps and basic threats; establishes core combat patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of tougher enemy types (Revenants, Barons); increased arena complexity; resource management intensifies
difficulty_curve.late_game: Heavy Arch-vile usage (TNT) or sustained hitscan pressure (Plutonia); boss encounters; The Plutonia Experiment maintains higher baseline difficulty
notable_spikes: Plutonia late-game sustained pressure compared to TNT's encounter-based design

notable_level_patterns.description: Large open areas with demon spawns; limited ammo forcing weapon choice discipline and kiting tactics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple TNT and Plutonia mid-game maps

notable_level_patterns.description: Arch-vile placement enables continuous resurrection of defeated demons, requiring aggressive demon elimination or Arch-vile neutralization priorities
Heavy concentration of Revenants and shotgunners in choreographed attack patterns; emphasizes movement and cover usage
notable_level_patterns.example_level: The Plutonia Experiment signature design philosophy

notable_level_patterns.description: Designed arena spaces for Cyberdemon and Spider Mastermind encounters with structured arena geometry

sources_used: Wikipedia - Final Doom article (gameplay mechanics, difficulty assessment, episode structure, enemy roster)
